# Role Encounter Table

Per-role canonical reading order across the project. One row per file (top-
level + segments), one column per role. Cell encoding:

- **integer** (e.g., `12`) — sequence number within this role's reading set
- **integer** + `*` (e.g., `12*`) — required at that position
- `+` — available in any order within its band
- `+*` — required, in any order within its band
- `—` — deliberately excluded for this role (priming-heavy; see the per-role notes file)
- blank — file not enumerated for this role

Segment-row suffixes:

- `[a]` — hoisted appendix segment, inserted after the segment that first surfaced it
- `[o]` — orphan: file in `src/` not referenced in OUTLINE.md (lint-outline flags these)

To regenerate: `ruby tools/role-encounter/bin/build-encounter-table`. To
pull a single role's view: `ruby tools/role-encounter/bin/role-view --role=NAME`.

Source-of-truth for what's in this table:

- `config/roles.yaml` — role definitions
- `config/top-level.yaml` — per-file role-membership and ordering
- `config/segments.yaml` — OUTLINE traversal rules (incl. appendix-hoist)


## Roles

- **`normal`** — Default contributor role. Agent is doing substantive work on the framework
  - audit-safe: `true` · README variant: `public` · segments: `true`
- **`de-novo-auditor`** — De-novo audit of the framework. Walks segments in OUTLINE order top-to-bottom
  - audit-safe: `false` · README variant: `auditor` · segments: `true`
- **`naming-voter`** — Multi-architecture naming-vote agent. Walks segments in OUTLINE order
  - audit-safe: `false` · README variant: `auditor` · segments: `true`
